headline: Anthropic removes Claude Code from Pro tier, reshaping AI tooling economics
body: Anthropic has removed Claude Code from its $20-per-month Pro subscription for new users, signaling a major shift in how the company packages AI coding capabilities. This move affects healthcare organizations evaluating Claude as part of clinical decision support, EHR integration, or administrative automation workflows. For healthcare AI consulting, the change raises questions about total cost of ownership and feature stability when building production systems on third-party models. Vendors and health systems currently relying on Claude Code for development tasks will need to reassess their subscription tier requirements and potentially renegotiate implementation costs. The silent removal of Opus 4.6 from Claude Code further underscores vendor platform instability as a real risk factor in healthcare AI procurement decisions.
